dataset: Piano Repertoire Explorer — display corpus digest
source_project: An alternative history of domestic piano repertoire, 1820–1910, reconstructed from national-library and music-trade catalogues (9 markets; Hofmeister/BnF+Gallica/Jisc/LoC/BNE/Norway/NLR).
core_caveat: publication ≠ sales ≠ playing. Every count is circulation evidence, not proof of popularity. The German trade (Hofmeister) is far better documented than other markets; absence of evidence in a thin market is not evidence of absence.

method_notes: Scores = weighted evidence events (deposits 1, trade listings 1, re-editions 3, arrangements 3, press 2, collections 4; anthology/canon signals weighted 0 in market rankings).
Rankings merge cross-language title families (e.g. Lieder ohne Worte = Songs without Words = Romances sans paroles).

Excluded from display (~12,300 rows): chamber duos & ensembles with piano, vocal songs/part-songs, complete-opera editions, pre-1820 library holdings, bound-volume shelf records, autograph manuscripts, Hofmeister Idem follow-on lines.
Early decades are thinly documented (5,260 works carry evidence 1829–33 vs 22,820 in 1873–77) — per-year rankings in early decades rest on far fewer records.
Canon-formation layer (635 anthology/revival selections: Trésor des pianistes, Peters Salon-Album, Pauer, Worlds Best Composers) is held separate from market counts by design.
